title: Quiz: Wiring and Filtering
description: Test your understanding of cryogenic wiring design, attenuation strategies, and filtering techniques for quantum computing systems

questions.question: Why is superconducting NbTi wire preferred for connections between the 4K stage and mixing chamber?

options.text: It has higher electrical conductivity than copper
It has essentially zero thermal conductivity below Tc, eliminating conducted heat
It is cheaper than other wire materials
It provides better shielding against electromagnetic interference

questions.explanation: Superconducting NbTi wire has essentially zero thermal conductivity below its critical temperature (~10K) because it carries no entropy. This eliminates conducted heat from warmer stages, which is critical for protecting the limited cooling power of the mixing chamber.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of placing attenuators at multiple temperature stages along microwave lines?

options.text: To increase the signal power at the qubit
To reduce the effective noise temperature seen by the qubit by attenuating thermal noise from warmer stages
To improve the impedance matching of the transmission line

questions.explanation: Attenuators reduce the effective noise temperature by attenuating thermal noise from warmer stages. Each cold attenuator defines a new noise floor at its local temperature. Multiple stages of attenuation are needed to reduce 300K noise to acceptable levels at the qubit.

questions.question: Why is infrared (IR) radiation from room temperature particularly problematic for superconducting qubits?

options.text: IR photons have much higher energy than qubit transitions and can break Cooper pairs
IR radiation increases the magnetic field
IR radiation only affects the 4K stage

questions.explanation: Room temperature IR photons (~30 THz, ~120 meV) have about 6000x more energy than typical qubit transitions (~5 GHz, ~20 ueV). These photons can break Cooper pairs, creating quasiparticles that cause qubit decoherence and energy relaxation.

questions.question: What type of filter is commonly used to block IR radiation while allowing microwave signals to pass?
questions.explanation: Eccosorb filters are made from magnetically-loaded silicone foam that absorbs electromagnetic radiation across a broad frequency range. They effectively block IR while providing controlled attenuation at microwave frequencies, making them ideal for cryogenic IR filtering.

questions.question: HEMT amplifiers are placed at the mixing chamber stage because they need to operate at the coldest possible temperature.
questions.explanation: False! HEMT amplifiers are placed at the 4K stage, not the mixing chamber. They dissipate 5-15 mW of power, which would overwhelm the mixing chamber's ~15 uW cooling capacity. The 4K stage can easily handle this heat load while still providing low noise temperature (2-5K).

questions.question: A 20 dB attenuator at the 4K stage receives -10 dBm of power. How much heat does it dissipate?
questions.explanation: Input power: -10 dBm = 100 uW. A 20 dB attenuator passes 1% of the power (10^(-20/10) = 0.01), so 99% is absorbed. Heat = 100 uW * 0.99 = 99 uW. This is why attenuator placement matters for thermal budgeting.

questions.question: Which of the following are valid reasons for using stainless steel coaxial cable between room temperature and 4K? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Low thermal conductivity reduces heat load
High signal attenuation helps with noise reduction

questions.explanation: Stainless steel coax has low thermal conductivity (a), provides attenuation that helps reduce thermal noise (b), and is mechanically robust (c). However, stainless steel is NOT superconducting (d) - it always has electrical resistance, which contributes to signal loss.
